messages.content: a quatrain about how a Sandclock sees time just running out, and forgets that it can flip to add more time

In grains, the sands of time slip through the glass,
Each dwindling moment draining from its breast,
Oblivious, the Sandclock measures past,
And with a flip, returns to sweet unrest.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time just running out, and forgets that it can flip to add more time

Through hourglass, the fleeting moments pour,
In narrow curves does dwindling time elapse,
Confined within the memories of yore,
Forgetting it can flip the sands to grasp.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time as finite , and forgets that with a flip it can make time  infinite

As sands cascade, the hourglass perceives,
A finite tale, within its fragile frame,
Yet, flipping endless cycles, it achieves
The art of making time a ceaseless game.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time as finite , and forgets that with a flip it can make time infinite

The hourglass beholds time's fleeting dance,
With every falling grain, an era gone,
Yet in its grasp—eternity's expanse,
A simple flip renews the endless dawn.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time as finite , and forgets that with a flip it can make time infinite

In narrow waist, the hourglass confines,
A finite view of time's relentless flow,
But with a gentle flip, its fate entwines,
The boundless realm where endless moments grow.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time as finite , and forgets that with a flip it can make time infinite

Fleeting moments pass, the hourglass sees,
A world confined by dwindling grains of sand,
Unknowing, with a flip, it holds the keys,
To endless time—a realm forever grand.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time as finite , and forgets that with a flip it can make time infinite

The hourglass gazes at time's ebb and flow,
In grains of sand, it thinks the story's told,
Yet flipping round, such boundless realm bestow,
A ceaseless dance in which the ages unfold.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time as finite , and forgets that with a flip it can make time infinite

Through glass, the hourglass eyes each fleeting breath,
Presuming finitude as grains descend,
Unmindful that, with just a twist from death,
Eternal realms of time may blend and bend.
a quatrain about how a hourglass sees time as finite , and forgets that with a flip it can make time infinite

Captured in glass, the hourglass observes,
A finite count as sands of time take flight,
Yet unaware, that with a flip, it serves,
An infinite expanse — time's endless night.
